---
date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY/MM/DD"
title: "28. Critical Values of the *t* Distribution"
subtitle: "Reference Table and Reproducible Generator"
reference-location: margin
resources:
  - ../../tables/make_t_critical_values_table.R
  - ../../tables/t_critical_values_extended.csv
  - ../../tables/t_critical_values_extended.tex
  - ../../tables/t_critical_values_extended.pdf
---

This page reproduces the classic printed table of critical values for the *t*
distribution, with degrees of freedom from $\nu = 1$ to $\nu = 1000$ and an
additional asymptotic row for $\nu = \infty$.

The continuation beyond 50 follows the same sampled degrees of freedom shown in
the printed reference table: even values to 100, then progressively wider
intervals through 1000.

The two header rows give equivalent tail areas:

- $\alpha(2)$ for the two-sided test,
- $\alpha(1)$ for the one-sided test.

The source files are available here:

- [R script](../../tables/make_t_critical_values_table.R)
- [CSV values](../../tables/t_critical_values_extended.csv)
- [LaTeX source](../../tables/t_critical_values_extended.tex)
- [PDF table](../../tables/t_critical_values_extended.pdf)

The table assets are committed to the repository and shipped as static site
resources, so they are not regenerated during a normal Quarto or Netlify build.
To rebuild them explicitly, run:

```bash
Rscript tables/make_t_critical_values_table.R
```

::: {.callout-important appearance="simple"}
## About this table

This table corresponds to a table of the same same by @zar1999biostatistical. It was created by taking a screeenshot of the table in the book, asking GPT-5.3-codex to create an R script to reproduce the table and typeset it in LaTeX.
:::

The PDF below is produced by the R script and typeset in LaTeX.

```{=html}
<object data="../../tables/t_critical_values_extended.pdf" type="application/pdf" width="100%" height="1400">
  <p>The embedded PDF could not be displayed here. Use the download links above.</p>
</object>
```
